title: SymmetricExtentDefinition Object
description: A definition object that is used to define the extents of a feature to be symmetric.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: create
methods.description: Statically creates a new SymmetricExtentDefinition object. This is used as input when create a new feature and defining the starting condition.

properties.name: distance
properties.description: Returns the current extent distance. If the SymmetricExtentDefinition object has been created statically and isn't associated with a feature this will return a ValueInput object. If the SymmetricExtentDefinition object is obtained from a feature this will return a ModelParameter object. You can use properties of the parameter to edit its value which will result in the feature updating.

properties.name: isFullLength
properties.description: Gets and sets if the distance defines the full extent length or half the length. A value of True indicates if defines the full length.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: parentFeature
properties.description: Returns the parent feature that this definition is associated with. If this definition has been created statically and is not associated with a feature this property will return null.

properties.name: taperAngle
properties.description: Returns the current taper angle. If the SymmetricExtentDefinition object has been created statically and isn't associated with a feature this will return a ValueInput object. If the SymmetricExtentDefinition object is obtained from a feature this will return a ModelParameter object. You can use properties of the parameter to edit its value which will result in the feature updating.

related_samples.name: Extrude Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new extrude feature.

classType.title: SymmetricExtentDefinition.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

create.title: SymmetricExtentDefinition.create Method
create.description: Statically creates a new SymmetricExtentDefinition object. This is used as input when create a new feature and defining the starting condition.
create.return_description: Returns the newly created SymmetricExtentDefinition or null in the case of a failure.

parameters.name: distance
parameters.description: An input ValueInput objects that defines either half the extent of the extrude or the full extent, depending on the value of the isFullLength argument.

parameters.name: isFullLength
parameters.description: An input boolean that specifies if the distance specified defines the full or half length of the extrusion.

distance.title: SymmetricExtentDefinition.distance Property
distance.description: Returns the current extent distance. If the SymmetricExtentDefinition object has been created statically and isn't associated with a feature this will return a ValueInput object. If the SymmetricExtentDefinition object is obtained from a feature this will return a ModelParameter object. You can use properties of the parameter to edit its value which will result in the feature updating.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = symmetricExtentDefinition_var.distance

isFullLength.title: SymmetricExtentDefinition.isFullLength Property
isFullLength.description: Gets and sets if the distance defines the full extent length or half the length. A value of True indicates if defines the full length.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = symmetricExtentDefinition_var.isFullLength

# Set the value of the property.
symmetricExtentDefinition_var.isFullLength = propertyValue

If the SymmetricExtentDefinition object is obtained from a feature this will return a ModelParameter object. You can use properties of the parameter to edit its value which will result in the feature updating.
